title: cornus sericea f. occidentalis
intro: Cornus sericea f. occidentalis, also known as Western Red Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to the western regions of North America, characterized by its bright red stems and clusters of white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Western Red Dogwood include its ability to promote cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and support immune function, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Western Red Dogwood can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for a range of herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
